member.name: T:BEQSAN.Api.Common.ApiResponse`1
member.summary: Canonical JSON envelope for every Result<T>-returning endpoint. Consumers (FRONT) can always rely on the same top-level shape regardless of success or failure — only , , and need to be checked.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Api.Common.ApiResponse`1.#ctor(System.Boolean,`0,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BEQSAN.Api.Common.ApiError})
member.summary: Canonical JSON envelope for every Result<T>-returning endpoint. Consumers (FRONT) can always rely on the same top-level shape regardless of success or failure — only , , and need to be checked.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Api.Common.ApiError
member.summary: One error entry in . Code is machine-readable English (stable across releases); Message is Georgian user-facing copy; Field is the camelCased input property name when bound; Metadata carries structured context the UI uses to render the error precisely (e.g. { "min": 60, "max": 140, "actual": 30 } for an out-of-range validation).

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Api.Common.ApiError.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: One error entry in . Code is machine-readable English (stable across releases); Message is Georgian user-facing copy; Field is the camelCased input property name when bound; Metadata carries structured context the UI uses to render the error precisely (e.g. { "min": 60, "max": 140, "actual": 30 } for an out-of-range validation).

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Api.Common.ResultExtensions.ToHttpResult(BEQSAN.Domain.Common.Result)
member.summary: Maps a Result to the canonical envelope. HTTP status comes from on failure; success returns 204.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Api.Common.ResultExtensions.ToHttpResult``1(BEQSAN.Domain.Common.Result{``0})
member.summary: Maps a Result<T> to the canonical envelope. 200 on success, ErrorType-mapped status on failure.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Api.Endpoints.CatalogSchemaAnchor
member.summary: Marker for Swashbuckle so the OpenAPI doc declares the response shapes even though minimal-API endpoints don't reference DTOs in their signatures directly. Used purely at codegen time.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Api.Endpoints.MetaWebhookEndpoints
member.summary: Meta webhook receiver. GET verifies the subscription (responds with the challenge); POST ingests events. NOT under the admin gate — Meta hits these directly. Authentication is via the hub.verify_token handshake and the standard X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC, which a follow-up will add once the Meta App is approved and the secret is in place.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Api.Endpoints.SocialEndpoints
member.summary: All under /api/v1/admin/social/* — gated by AdminTokenAuthMiddleware. Two read endpoints (accounts, inbox) and the rest write/mutate.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Api.Middleware.AdminTokenAuthMiddleware
member.summary: Phase 0 admin gate — checks X-Admin-Token against Social:AdminToken from configuration. Bypasses the public catalog and configurator routes; only paths under /api/v1/admin/ require the header. Replaced by JWT-bearer auth once the auth feature ships (see [docs/questions.md] entry: „admin auth: Phase 2 — proper JWT").

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Api.Middleware.AdminTokenAuthMiddleware.#ctor(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.RequestDelegate,Microsoft.Extensions.Options.IOptions{BEQSAN.Infrastructure.Social.SocialOptions})
member.summary: Phase 0 admin gate — checks X-Admin-Token against Social:AdminToken from configuration. Bypasses the public catalog and configurator routes; only paths under /api/v1/admin/ require the header. Replaced by JWT-bearer auth once the auth feature ships (see [docs/questions.md] entry: „admin auth: Phase 2 — proper JWT").
